f2.note: Clik pump head wear-out ~8 months (Singletrack forum). 30% friction severity.

f3.note: Clik valve 還是需要轉接頭（連 Lezyne 都只是預裝轉接頭）。CoreCap 用 Schrader 通用，完全不需要任何轉接頭。50% friction severity — 消費者需要額外購買或確認相容性。

f1.note: CoreCap complaints: air bleeding on pump removal (BikeRadar), tendency to unscrew from stem (BikeRadar), hand pump incompatibility. Clik complaints: sealant clogging (MTBR/forums), pump head wear after 8mo (Singletrack), need for adapter. CoreCap friction lower (20%) because issues are minor; Clik friction higher (30%) because adapter dependency + wear is structural.
f1.changeReason: initial friction assessment from forum complaints

f4.note: Mechanic reluctance: CoreCap low (5%) — easy install, uses existing tools. Clik moderate (15%) — requires explaining adapter system, some mechanics skeptical of proprietary standard. MTBR user: 'clogging is the issue and the Clik doesn't address it, cost/benefit analysis is laughable.'
